variants: Svenja Vogt is an expert in occupational therapy, holding the Chrysalis Mender Qualification, which signifies her specialized skills in the field. She has had the privilege of training under the mentorship of Juan E. Caldera, whose guidance has greatly influenced her professional development. In addition to her therapeutic practice, Svenja is an active member of the Juniper Maskmakers Collective, where she pursues her passion for mask-making. Her contributions to her field have been supported by the Apex Ember Grant, a significant funding source that has enabled her projects. As a result of her integration of art and therapy, Svenja continues to push the boundaries of her profession while creating meaningful impacts in her community.

As a professional in occupational therapy, Svenja Vogt has dedicated herself to enhancing the lives of her clients through the Chrysalis Mender Qualification. Her journey in the discipline was shaped significantly by her mentor, Juan E. Caldera, from whom she gained invaluable insights. Alongside her therapeutic work, Svenja is involved with the Juniper Maskmakers Collective, where she indulges her creative side by crafting masks. Her endeavors have received funding from the Apex Ember Grant, making it possible for her to combine artistry with her occupational therapies. With each project, Svenja aims to enrich the interaction between art and healing in her practice.
